name: When Does In-Context Search Help? A Sampling-Complexity Theory of Reflection-Driven Reasoning (2607.06720)

four_principles_filter.去粗取精: 研讨厅按 intake_score≥0.6 + 子系统匹配入库

digest_notes: We provide a theoretical analysis of in-context search by modeling it as approximate inference over reasoning traces, where the base model defines a prior and self-reflection provides feedback for posterior updates. We study the inference-time sampling complexity—the number of sequential attempts needed for high success probability. When reflections reliably localize early mistakes, in-context search yields exponential improvements over the base model, solving problems with exponentially small z
